name: libemail-valid-perl
title: libemail-valid-perl
summary: Perl module for checking the validity of Internet email addresses
description:
Perl module for checking the validity of Internet email addresses
Email::Valid determines whether an email address is well-formed, and
optionally, whether a mail host exists for the domain.
.
Please note that there is no way to determine whether an address targets
a valid recipient without actually attempting to deliver a message.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libemail-valid-perl because Perl module for checking the validity of Internet email addresses.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libemail-valid-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libemail-valid-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libemail-valid-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
